stories.title: Room-temperature device synchronizes distant laser spots into single coherent 'supermode'
stories.desc: Researchers have demonstrated a new way to make spatially separated lasers synchronize and act as a single coherent light source—without extreme conditions or complex materials.

stories.title: Modeling nuclear fusion at lightning speed
stories.desc: As we scour and scorch the Earth for deeper wells of energy, investors and government agencies are pouring billions into nuclear fusion research. The hope is that fusion may ultimately provide a virtually limitless source of clean energy.

stories.title: Broken time-reversal symmetry phase in kagome metals may establish conditions for superconductivity
stories.desc: Physicists have long suspected that a peculiar quantum state lurks inside a class of materials known as kagome metals, but proving its existence has been elusive. Now, a team led by Yeongkwan Kim at the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology has performed experiments on a kagome metal…

stories.title: Electron-Ion Collider's radiofrequency controls system passes first real-world test
stories.desc: The U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Brookhaven National Laboratory has reached a key early milestone in developing radiofrequency control systems for the Electron-Ion Collider (EIC)—a next-generation research facility that will collide electrons with ions to reveal how the building blocks of…

stories.title: Investigating quantum and molecular plumbing in nanofluidics research
stories.desc: Our body contains an intricate system of tiny vessels through which blood, water and other molecules flow. When the size of the pipes shrinks to the nanoscale, where only a few molecules can fit side by side, the classical laws of physics governing the behavior of water are influenced by the atomic…

stories.title: A Dark Dimension Could Link Two of the Universe’s Great Unknowns
stories.desc: Recent observations suggest that dark energy is changing over time. Theorists wonder if dark matter is, too. The post A Dark Dimension Could Link Two of the Universe’s Great Unknowns first appeared on Quanta Magazine

stories.title: Experiment upends beliefs on how electrons actually behave in warm dense matter
stories.desc: Researchers at European XFEL, Helmholtz-Zentrum Dresden-Rossendorf (HZDR), Rostock University and other collaborating institutions have used high-precision experiments to demonstrate that the most widely used models for the behavior of electrons in warm dense matter are inaccurate. Warm dense…

stories.title: A minimal model for how a cell takes shape from the inside
stories.desc: Researchers at the University of Twente and Utrecht University have packed rigid, rod-shaped particles into soft lipid containers the size of a living cell and watched the container and its contents reshape each other. The vesicle's form determines how the rods line up; the tightly packed rods, in…

stories.title: A Quiet Quantum Revolution in Earth’s Deep Interior
stories.desc: A subtle change in iron ions’ electronic configuration produces a measurable difference in seismic wave speeds through mantle rocks.
